text: Databases are essential for storing and organising data. Relational databases, like SQL, use tables with rows and columns, similar to a spreadsheet. To retrieve information, you write queries using structured query language. NoSQL databases, on the other hand, store data in more flexible formats, such as documents or key-value pairs, which is useful for large-scale or unstructured data. Before creating a database, it is important to model your data well — deciding how different pieces of information relate to each other. Finally, indexes can speed up searches by allowing the database to find data faster, rather than scanning every row or record.
